article.title: OSHA updates Respiratory Protection guidance for healthcare settings
article.summary: OSHA's revised guidance on 29 CFR 1910.134 emphasizes annual fit-testing for N95 respirators in healthcare settings and clarifies medical evaluation timing. Practices should ensure all clinical staff using respirators have current fit-test records.
article.rawContent: OSHA's updated Respiratory Protection healthcare guidance reaffirms the annual fit-test requirement for tight-fitting respirators (including N95s) under 29 CFR 1910.134(f). The guidance addresses common practice misconceptions: a single fit test does not satisfy the annual requirement; a change in respirator model, weight loss/gain of 20+ pounds, or facial structure change (dental work, scarring) requires re-testing. Medical evaluations under 29 CFR 1910.134(e) must be completed before fit-testing and should be repeated when a healthcare professional recommends.
